description:
The configured concurrent worker cap is currently being ignored during runs. A configuration of `2` still allowed roughly `6` workers to spin up and run simultaneously.

Scope:
- trace how configured concurrency is loaded and enforced in `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/config.ts`, `src/orchestrator.ts`, and any worker-launch path
- ensure the runtime honors the configured limit consistently
- add regression coverage that proves the orchestrator will not exceed the configured worker count
- verify the fix against the real execution path, not only a config parser unit test

Definition of done:
- tests added/updated under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`
- `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
title: Respect configured concurrent worker limits during beadwork runs
